experiment_list: manually collected 1282 raw samples from 126 unique scientific articles with descriptions of synthesis, materials characterizations, and measurements of magnetic hyperthermia and MRI performances in vitro
calculated average magnetic moment of metals in the core using magnetic moments of each element with help of stoichiometry
obtained average spin of metal elements on the surface to describe interactions between the surface elements and proton spins responsible for relaxation properties of the nanoparticle
employed a partition coefficient LogP and number of H acceptors, which were calculated using SMILES representations of organic molecules
calculated volume and surface area using the shape and size of the nanoparticle to describe morphological characteristics of nanomagnets
calculated and analyzed the Pearson correlation matrix
reduced a group of geometrical features with high cross-correlations to a single area-volume feature
removed the total of 211 samples such that each sample in the remaining dataset had no more than 15% of missing values
applied the kNN imputation algorithm to fill the gaps and obtain the complete dataset
transformed highly correlated geometrical features into area to volume and max to min length ratios reflecting size and shape anisotropy of nanoparticles
discarded noninformative features of low variance, i.e., coercivity and remanent magnetization values for prediction of MRI relaxivities
removed samples with outliers by setting empirical thresholds on feature values while analyzing their distributions
divided the final dataset into three, one for each regression problem: prediction of SAR value, r1 or r2 relaxivity containing 460, 355, and 465 samples, respectively
normalized and log-scaled the data for each problem before splitting to train and test sets in the 80/20 ratio
optimized and compared performance of four models using the model-agnostic Optuna framework
selected the best model using the following metrics: mean Q2 and RMSE for tenfold cross-validation and R2 and RMSE for test samples
examined the performance of the selected models on the test sets
plotted SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) values for each selected ML model
formed another testing set of samples that have not been used for training and optimizing hyperparameters of the ML models
collected some validation samples from the most recent experimental works published in 2023
compared model predictions and experimental measurements of log-scaled SAR, r1 and r2 relaxivities
computed percent of predictions that differed from the true values by less than 50%
plotted predicted and experimental values over the field amplitude at different frequencies for cubic Fe3O4 nanoparticles of SAR validation samples
plotted predictions of r2 relaxivities for Fe3O4 nanoparticles at different sizes
